title: The One With All The Haste

lines.text: Rachel's bedroom; Rachel is awoken by a man singing in the next apartment.

lines.speaker: The Singing Man
lines.text: (singing, duh) Morning's here! Sunshine is here! The sky is clear, the morning's here! The morning's here!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: HEY!! Do you have to do that? It's Saturday!

lines.speaker: The Singing Man
lines.text: Oh come on! Morning's here! (Starts singing) Morning's here! The morning is here! Sunshine is here!
Rachel slams shut her window and storms into the living room, where Joey and Monica are eating breakfast.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I hate this apartment! I hate the color of these walls! I hate the fact that this place still smells like bird! I hate that singing guy!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you kidding? I love that guy! (Starts singing) Morning's here! Morning is here-

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Stop it! I will kill you. I hate the fact that my room is so small.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, I have all the space I need. Just do what I did.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica, you don't even have a bed, you sleep in a ball on the floor!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know what? I am really tired of your bellyaching! Okay, I-I worked really hard at making this a nice place for us to live!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm sorry. I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: See, this is a great apartment.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Shut up! This place is a hole!
Opening Credits
Ross's bedroom; Ross and Emily are making out. Ross as a new feature.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Oh, blimey, I still can't believe you've got an earring!
Yep, it's a little gold loop.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh? I know, I know. Who am I? David Bowe? (Shakes his head around, pretending like he's jamming.)

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: He does that?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, I don't know, whatever.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I think it makes you look really dangerous.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I know. Y'know what, I never would've gotten this if it weren't for you. No really, when I'm with you I'm-I'm like this whole other guy, I love that guy! I mean, I love you too, a lot, but that guy! I-I love that guy!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I love both of you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?
They kiss.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I wish I didn't have to go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Then don't. Stay here. Just don't go so soon to London, just one more day.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Ohh, Ross, please!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: One more day, seriously/

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Don't do this to me, again. You'd know I'd stay here in a minute, but I'd really miss so much work, they'll fire me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, then you can stay as long as you want.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I wish I could.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh no. Don't, don't, don't start packing. Come on! (She puts some clothes into her bag, and Ross throws them out.)

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I don't think you understand packing. Look, I just don't want to leave it to the last minute. Last time I left in such a rush, I left my knickers here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I know, I uh, I tried them on.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: You didn't!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No. No, I didn't. I didn't want to be that guy.
Monica and Rachel's erm, Chandler and Joey's, Joey and Chandler are watching one of those Kung Fu movies and imitating the moves.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering, wearing Santa pants) Hello!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ho! Ho! Ho!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Excuse me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Your pants!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yeah! You like 'em? I just, I went to a used clothes store and got a bunch of maternity stuff. These are sooo comfortable!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh, Pheebs, those are uh, those are Santa pants.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Santa pants. (Phoebe still doesn't get it.) Santa Claus's pants.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nuh-uh! They're maternity pants. They even came with a list of baby names. (Pulls out a sheet of paper which lists who's been naughty and who's been nice.) See, these names are good, and these names are bad. (Finally, she figures it out.) Ohh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So-Hey, Pheebs! So, how are the elves?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't know! How are the-the-the-the, y'know-You're clothes aren't funny.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, guys, what-what should I wear to a Knicks game?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uhh, a T-shirt that says, "I don't belong here."

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You have Knicks tickets?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, my mom got my dad's season tickets in the divorce, so she just gave them to me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, apparently, they're pretty good seats.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (examining the tickets) Oh my God! Those are almost right on the floor!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you guys want these?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah we do!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, well you got 'em.

lines.speaker: Both
lines.text: All right!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Just give us our apartment back!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Boy! I didn't see that coming!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you serious?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, come on! We know what these are worth.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, what, do you think we're stupid?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You're not stupid. You're meaner than I thought.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What do you say?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Forget it! Okay, I'm not giving up my bachelor pad for some basketball seats!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You're bachelor pad?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Have you even had a girl up here?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. But uh, Joey has, and I usually talk to them in the morning time.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, you do!
Central Perk, Joey is whining to Chandler about the tickets.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (ignoring him) Yes, Gunther, can I get two cups of chino, please?

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Good one.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on, season tickets! Season tickets, do you know what that means?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Forget it! Okay, I'm not giving up the apartment.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh come-look, when I was a kid my dad's company gave season tickets to the number one salesman every year, all right? My dad never won! Of course, he wasn't in the sales division, but still, I never ever, ever forgot that!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey, guys! (They both notice his new little friend)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We don't make enough fun of you already?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah, Emily convinced me to do it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You do know that Wham broke up?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I like it, and Emily likes it, and that's what counts. So uh, how are you guys doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-no, don't try and talk all normal with that thing in your ear.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Where is Emily?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ugh, she's saying good-bye to her uncle.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Man, didn't she like just get here?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!! Yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Easy tiger.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I just, I hate this so much! I mean, every time I go pick her up at the airport, it's-it's so great. But at the same time I'm thinking, "Well, I'm gonna be right back there in a couple of days, dropping her off."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So what are you going to do?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nothing! There's nothing to do! I mean, she lives there, I live here. I mean, she-she'd have to uh, move here. She should move here!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I could ask her to live with me!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you serious?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I mean, why not! I mean, I mean why not?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because you've only known her for six weeks! Okay, I've got a carton of milk in my fridge I've had a longer relationship with!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look guys, when I'm with her it's-it's-it's like she brings this-this-this great side out of me. I mean I-I-I love her, y'know?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And I love the milk! But, I'm not gonna some British girl to move in with me! (Realizes that made no sense.) Joey, you say things now.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right look, Ross, he's right. Emily's great, she's great! But this way too soon, you're only gonna scare her!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't want to do that.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! You don't want to wreck it, you don't want to go to fast!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, no, you're right, I know, you're right, I'm not, I'm not gonna do it. All right, thanks guys. (Gets up to leave.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, no problem, just remember to wake us up before you go-go.
Monica and Rachel's erm, Chandler and Joey's, later that same day. Joey and Chandler are eating pizza, and Phoebe is trying to knit something.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's too hard. Too hard!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering with Rachel) All right boys, last chance for the tickets!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Or I'll give them to my new boyfriend, Joshua.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wait-wait-wait-wait! (To Chandler) Come on! Come on, let's trade! The timing's perfect, I just clogged the toilet!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, I want those basketball seats as much as you do! Okay, but we can't leave in the small apartment after we've lived here! Didn't you ever read Flowers for Algernon?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes! Didn't you ever read Sports Illustrated?! No! I didn't read yours! But come on, we can go to the game tonight!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, the only way I will even consider this is if they offer a lot more than just season seats.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's the Knicks!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Screw the Knicks!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I didn't mean that. I just meant that the apartment is worth so much more.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And the Knicks rule all.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, the Knicks rule all!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, so? Are you gonna do it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. No. We're not gonna do that, y'know why? Because its not an even trade.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, okay, look, what if you could keep the apartment and get the tickets?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Done!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Let me finish.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm talking about a bet, winner takes all.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh, we could end up with nothing.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Or you could end up with everything.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh, I like that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, so what do you say?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, just do it!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Op, op, I'm convinced!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on man, you know I'd do it for you! Because, you're my best friend.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, but you can't use that again for a whole year. I'm in.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, this is so exciting! Ooh, God, what are you going to bet?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, okay, well, I think we should let Phoebe decide, because she's the only who's impartial, and she's so pretty.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Umm, ooh, ooh-oh, I have a game!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: This is great!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's the game?! What's the game?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, well, it doesn't have a name-oh, okay, Phoebeball! No, it doesn't have a name. Umm, okay, Monica, what is your favourite thing about trees?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They're green?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good! Good! Five points!
They both rejoice; Chandler is totally confused.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, Joey, same question.
He looks to Chandler, who doesn't have a clue.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh, they're tall.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, three points. Both fine answers, but we were looking for leafy, leafy.
Joey turns and is angry that Chandler didn't come up with the answer.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's not even a game!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? Shut up! We're winning!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You wanna finish this right now? All right, we get a deck of cards, high card wins. What do you say?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fine, let's do it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I have cards!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, good.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! Here! (She grabs a deck out of her purse) Oh no, these are the trick deck. Okay. Here yes. Okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, you guys uh, you guys pick first

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. (She picks a card.) Four.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's a low one!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! Okay. (Joey picks a card.) Phoebe, you look, I can't.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What make you think I can?! (Shields her eyes from it.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. Okay. (He looks at the card.) Ace!
Both Joey and Chandler and Monica and Rachel jump up and down for joy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why are you screaming and hugging?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because we won our apartment back!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? Ace is high! Jack, queen, king, ace!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! Ace is low! Ace, two, three, four!
They all look to Phoebe to settle this.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't know. Ooh! Ooh! Look it! (She fans out the trick deck.) Ah-ha!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, cut, let's pick again, pick again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on apartment! Come on apartment! (Picks a card.) Oh! I know queen is high!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh-huh, not as high as...(picks a card) It worked! King!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah baby!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But, we pick again! We pick again!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Tickets please! (Rachel hands over the tickets) That's courtside baby!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Seriously, good game though. Good game. (He tries to congratulate them, but they pull away.) (To Chandler) What are they so mad about? They get the apartment back!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No they didn't!
Ross's apartment, Emily has packed as Ross returns.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I packed while you were gone. I left some knickers under your pillow.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (laughs) Move in with me.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Don't be scared, I-I know it sounds crazy and-and people will say it's too soon, but just-just think, think how great it will be.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Ohh, no. Ugh. Oh, leaving London, my whole family lives there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: My job!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, so, you-you'll get a job here! I mean, I'm always hearing about uh, them foreigners coming in here and stealing American jobs; that could be you!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Yeah, but it-it-it's my whole life-you come to England.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I can't. I would, I really would, but my son is here; I can't leave him. Isn't-you don't think there's any way?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Ohh, I don't think so. I mean it would be different if it was way into the future -and-and-and we were getting married or something.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Oh no, no, right I shouldn't have said married. Uh, please don't go freaky on me. I didn't mean it. Well, I didn't say it; I take it back!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no, don't. Why don't we?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Why don't we what?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Get married.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: You are mad!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No! I'm not! It's-it's-it's perfect! I mean it's better than you just-just moving here, 'cause it's us together forever, and that's-that's what I want.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: We've only known each other for six weeks!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I know, so what? I mean, who's-who's to say? Does that me we-we can't do it? Look, huh, I was with Carol for four years before we got married and I wound up divorced from a pregnant lesbian. I mean, this, this makes sense for us. Come on! I mean, on our first date we ended up spending the whole weekend in Vermont! I mean, last night I got my ear pierced! Me! This feels right. Doesn't it?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: My parents are going to be really mad.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Is that-are you saying yes? Is that yes?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Yes.
They kiss and hug.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes! We're getting married?!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: We're getting married!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come here, come here. Uh, (He takes the earring out.) ow! Emily, will you marry me?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Yes.
He tries to put it on her finger.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Ohh, it's a bit small.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Damn! I thought that was going to be romantic as hell!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: It was.
They kiss.
Commerical Break
The hallway, Joey and Chandler are coming back from the game.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Those were like the best seats ever.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah. Hey! Should we give these shirts to the girls? Y'know, kinda like a peace offering.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, that's very nice. Plus, y'know they were free and they're too small.

He knocks on the girls' door and walks in. Surprise! The girls, obviously using Star Trek technology, have completely moved everything in both apartments back to their original positions, all in the time it took for the guys to go to a basketball game. Wow! Anyhoo, Chandler is stunned, and Joey doesn't even realise it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh. Oh, God! (He starts running around like a chicken with his head cut off.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, want a beer? (Hands him a beer and sits down in one of the chairs.) (Jumping up.) WHOA!!!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I KNOW!!!
They both sprint to what used to be their apartment.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Open up! Open up! Open up!
A very angry Monica opens the door with the security chain still on.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We'll discuss it, in the morning! (Slams the door shut.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What the hell is going on?!
It's Rachel's turn to open the door.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We took our apartment back!! (Slams the door shut.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (opening the door) I had nothing to do with it. (Closes the door.) (Opens the door.) Okay, it was my idea, but I don't feel good about it.
She goes to close the door, but Chandler puts his foot it in.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We are switching back, right now!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, we're not! We're not leaving!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you're gonna have to leave sometime, because you both have jobs, and as soon as you do, we're switching it back! There's nothing you can do to stop us! Right, Joe?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't want to move again!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't care, this is our apartment! And they stole-you stole it-our apartment, and we won that apartment fair and square, twice! And I am getting it back right now. I'm getting back right now!
They open the door.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right. We figured you might respond this way, so we have a backup offer.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no-no-no, no more offers. You can't offer anything to us!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Let us keep the apartment and...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: As a thank you, Rachel and I will kiss for one minute.
[Time lapse. The guys are entering their apartment.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Totally worth it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That was one good minute!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Good night.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Good night.
They both go back into their old rooms and shut the doors. Of course, Chandler has to close both sections of his door.
[Cut to the girls apartment.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Men are such idiots.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! Can you believe that something that stupid actually got us our apartment back?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's so funny to think if you'd just done that right after the last contest, no one would have had to move at all.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, let-let-let's pretend that's not true.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, scarf's done. (It's not really a scarf, it's just a bunch of yarn that Phoebe has tied together. Just then, Ross and Emily enter dragging with them Joey and Chandler.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on! Come on. Come on.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What-what's going on?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross has some big thing to tell everyone.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uhh, okay, it's uh, Emily and I, we decided to uh, to get married.
The gang is stunned.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What? Oh, are you pregnant too?!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Umm, no.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: When, when did-how, how did you...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We, we just decided to uh, to go for it.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I mean, we know it's a bit hasty but, uh, it just feels so right, so...
Rachel slowly walks in from her bedroom. She is stunned speechless.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (turning around.) Umm, uh, I was just telling the guys...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I-I heard. (Pause, everyone looks at each other, waiting for Rachel's reaction.) I think it's great! (Hugs Ross.) Ohh, I'm so happy for you!
Seeing Rachel's apparently okay with this, the rest of the gang jumps up to congratulate Ross and Emily on their pending nuptials.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, well, that's great!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! Yeah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Ross) I can't believe you're getting married!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. (They hug again.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Monica and Rachel made out. (Giggles like a schoolboy and Monica glares at him.)
Closing Credits
Joey's bedroom, he is awoken by the singing guy.

lines.speaker: The Singing Man
lines.text: (singing) Morning's here! The morning's here!
Joey joins him.

lines.speaker: Both
lines.text: Sunshine is here! The sky is clear, the morning's here!

lines.speaker: The Singing Man
lines.text: Hey! You're back!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! (Singing) Get into gear!

lines.speaker: The Singing Man
lines.text: (singing) Breakfast is near!

lines.speaker: Both
lines.text: The dark of night has disappeared!!

lines.speaker: The Singing Man
lines.text: I'll see you tomorrow morning!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (happily) Okay!
End
